## [00:00 - 03:04]

Alright, I'm going to show you guys this new thing called lifetime value for lookalikes. So you'll find this under the audience tab of your ad account under audiences. So if you just go in business manager, click on this. And if you've got it, you'll see this new sort of promotion of it. So we're going to click get started. Now lifetime value is how much a customer spends or is worth to you over the period of the lifetime that they do business with you. So even though a customer might only be paying $20 for your deodorant one time, the lifetime value of that customer may be $300 because they're going to end up buying five or six or ten or twenty deodorants from you over the next few years. So same with things like dental offices, gyms. Typically those types of businesses have high lifetime values, subscription businesses where your customer is not necessarily just paying one fee, one product with you, but they're going to be buying multiple different products over the course of their, I don't want to say career, but over the course of the relationship with that customer. So essentially that's what lifetime value is. So what we're going to do is click get started. We're going to accept the terms. And you'll see here this looks almost exactly the same as creating a normal custom audience based on data, except what we're going to do here is it doesn't let you copy and paste. So that's the only difference. We're going to upload a file and you can download the template here. And you'll see here. So it's got email, phone number, name, and then the difference here is the value. So what Facebook is going to do when you upload this, Facebook is going to pick out the ones with the highest lifetime values. So this one, this one, and this one and try to create look likes or audiences that are most similar to the higher value worth customers on your list. Right. So obviously this requires a bit of CRM. You need to upload this data or you need to find this data. If you've been using tools like HubSpot or infusion soft, you should be able to pull out a lifetime value. Otherwise you need to manually input this. So I'm just going to delete this and I'm going to upload my file. Okay. So I've uploaded the file. I'm going to click next and it's going to ask me to set or choose the column that has the value. So this is this one. And then you'll see here. First name, email. This is very, very similar to what we normally do.

## [03:04 - 03:46]

We're going to go upload and create. Now we can create a look like from this. So we can select the United States and create the academy. And now we've got a look like audience. It's still populating. Allow about an hour for this. It'll look like audience of our highest valued lifetime customers or customers with the highest lifetime value. And it's pretty much that simple.
